questions.question_text: In South Dakota, the entry-level real estate license is called a:
questions.explanation: South Dakota uses the term 'broker associate' for the entry-level license, which is equivalent to 'salesperson' in most other states. This unique terminology is a common source of exam confusion.

questions.question_text: Which South Dakota real estate license requires NO education or examination?
questions.explanation: The residential rental agent license is unique in SD — it requires only an application approval with no education or exam. By contrast, broker associates need 116 hours, property managers need 40 hours, and auctioneers need 116 hours plus an exam.

questions.question_text: A person wants to manage rental properties in South Dakota. How many hours of education are required for the property management license?
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires a separate property management license with 40 hours of Fundamentals of Property Management education. This is different from the 116-hour broker associate requirement and the residential rental agent license (which needs no education).

questions.question_text: To become a responsible broker in South Dakota, what is the minimum period of active broker associate status required?
questions.explanation: A responsible broker applicant must have at least 2 years of active broker associate status. They must also complete the 116-hour pre-licensing course plus a 24-hour Responsible Broker Course and pass the broker exam at 75%.

questions.question_text: A broker associate in South Dakota wants to upgrade to responsible broker. What additional course must they complete?
questions.explanation: Beyond the standard 116-hour pre-licensing course and 2 years of active experience, a responsible broker applicant must complete a 24-hour Responsible Broker Course and pass the broker exam at 75%.

questions.question_text: A broker associate candidate passes the state exam on March 1. By what date must they submit their license application?
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires license applications to be submitted within 60 days of passing the last exam portion. Missing this deadline may require re-examination.

questions.question_text: How many classroom hours of pre-licensing education must a South Dakota broker associate candidate complete?
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires 116 classroom hours of approved pre-licensing education for broker associate candidates. This is higher than many states' salesperson requirements, reflecting SD's comprehensive entry-level preparation.

questions.question_text: A broker associate candidate is preparing their budget. What is the non-refundable application fee?
questions.explanation: The SD broker associate application fee is $225 and is non-refundable. The exam fee is $98 per section. These costs are in addition to education and background check fees ($43.25 for fingerprinting).

questions.question_text: Which must a broker associate obtain BEFORE receiving their South Dakota license?
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires E&O insurance before licensure — it's a prerequisite, not something obtained afterward. Candidates must also complete background checks (state and federal fingerprints) before the license is issued.

questions.question_text: What minimum passing score is required on both the national and state portions of the SD real estate exam?
questions.explanation: Both the national portion (90 items) and state portion (52 items) require a minimum 75% passing score. The exam is split — each portion is scored independently, and only a failed portion needs to be retaken.

questions.question_text: A new broker associate must complete how many total hours of postlicensing education?
questions.explanation: New broker associates must complete 60 total hours of postlicensing education: 30 hours in the first 2 license years (Period 1) and 30 hours in the next 2 years (Period 2). This is separate from ongoing CE requirements.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours must a South Dakota licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: SD licensees need 24 hours of CE per 2-year renewal cycle, with at least 12 hours in mandatory subject areas (contracts, license law, fair housing, ethics, etc.). The biennial renewal fee is $125.

questions.question_text: A licensee forgets to renew by the November 30 deadline. What late penalty applies?
questions.explanation: South Dakota charges a $20 per month late renewal penalty after the November 30 expiration date. All SD real estate licenses expire on November 30 of the renewal year. The standard renewal fee is $125.

questions.question_text: Of the 24 hours of CE required per cycle, how many must be in mandatory subject areas?
questions.explanation: At least 12 of the 24 required CE hours must cover mandatory topics including real estate contracts, license law, fair housing, ethics, environmental issues, antitrust, ADA, brokerage services, and property management.

questions.question_text: On what date do all South Dakota real estate licenses expire?
questions.explanation: All SD real estate licenses share a common expiration date of November 30. This uniform expiration simplifies administration for the SDREC. Renewal requires 24 hours of CE and a $125 biennial fee.
